# Transcrição: How_to_Express_Yourself_Better

- **Fonte:** How_to_Express_Yourself_Better
- **Duração:** 01:49
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:49]

I get this question a lot. Vin, how do I get better at expressing myself? And to answer this, I have to share with you what I saw at a friend's birthday party. Well, it was for his kids. But I was there and his name's Finn. And I watched Finn walk up to mum and I'm Celeste. Mum, can I have some chocolates? And mum said, No, you've had enough chocolates for the day. And I watched Finn then walk up to his dad, who's Lenny, one of my good friends. Dad, can I have more chocolates, please? And the dad immediately just took a chocolate and said, You're body, of course. And I thought that was brilliant. Children are so smart. And what's even more interesting is I watched the other kids watch Finn. And the other kids learned from that interaction immediately. None of them went to their mums. They all immediately went to their dads and every single dad fell for it immediately and they just gave their kids chocolate. Human beings are a remarkable species. We learn so much from watching other people. So when it comes to you getting better at expressing yourself, I can almost guarantee you that there are great communicators who already exist in your circle. All you have to do is pay attention to how they express themselves. How do they use their voice? What kind of language do they use? Do they use their facial expressions? Do they use their hand gestures? All you have to do is pay more attention to what they're doing. Then from those interactions, you're going to pick up some clues. And then you have to do what the other kids did once they learned from Finn. They practiced it and they went to their own fathers and asked them for chocolate. That's what you're going to have to do too. As you learn these little behavioral traits that the great communicators around you exhibit, you then have to take those traits and practice them. So look out for the fins in your world. Look out for the great communicators. Look out for those who are great at expressing themselves and model their behavior. And more importantly, again, practice those behaviors. Don't feel like you have to learn everything on your own. You can learn from others.
